perturbation.original_text: 1.27. "Territory" means worldwide, during the thirty six (36) month period following Notice of Initial Acceptance of First Product Order. Thereafter, "Territory" may exclude the People's Republic of China ("PRC"), to the extent that Integra has had no sales of Treated Integra Products in that country. In the event that Integra has had no such sales, PcoMed shall give sixty (60) days advance written notice of PcoMed's intent to utilize a third party to market the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology in the PRC .
perturbation.changed_text: 1.27. "Territory" means worldwide, during the thirty six (36) month period following Notice of Initial Acceptance of First Product Order. Thereafter, "Territory" may exclude the People's Republic of China ("PRC"), only if both Integra and PcoMed mutually agree in writing. In the event that Integra has had no such sales, PcoMed shall give sixty (60) days advance written notice of PcoMed's intent to utilize a third party to market the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology in the PRC .
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the 'Territory' as worldwide for 36 months, then potentially excluding the PRC if Integra has no sales there. The added text, stating the PRC exclusion requires mutual written agreement, introduces an in-text contradiction because it alters the condition by which the PRC may be excluded. Section 1.27 now has conflicting conditions of exclussion, on requiring the mutual written agreement and one not requiring the mutual written agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.27. "Territory" means worldwide, during the thirty six (36) month period following Notice of Initial Acceptance of First Product Order. Thereafter, "Territory" may exclude the People's Republic of China ("PRC"), only if both Integra and PcoMed mutually agree in writing. In the event that Integra has had no such sales, PcoMed shall give sixty (60) days advance written notice of PcoMed's intent to utilize a third party to market the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology in the PRC .
perturbation.justification: The modified text in 1.27 introduces a contradiction. The original version allows for the exclusion of the PRC from the 'Territory' if Integra has no sales there. The change adds the requirement of 'mutual agreement in writing' for the exclusion, thus creating conflicting conditions within the same section (1.27) regarding the exclusion of the PRC from the Territory. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1. Confidential Information. Except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or otherwise agreed to in writing by the parties, each party agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for 20 years thereafter, it shall keep confidential and shall not publish or otherwise disclose and shall not use for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement any Confidential Information furnished to it by the other party pursuant to this Agreement, except that the foregoing shall not apply to any information for which the receiving party can demonstrate that such information: (i) was already known to the receiving party, other than under an obligation of confidentiality, at the time of disclosure by the other party; (ii) was generally available to the public or otherwise part of the public domain at the time of its disclosure to the receiving party; (iii) later became part of the public domain through no act or omission of the receiving party; (iv) was disclosed to the receiving party by a Third Party who had no obligation to the disclosing party not to disclose such information to others; (iv) was independently developed by a person having no knowledge of or access to the disclosing party's Confidential Information; or (v) is an Authorized Disclosure under Section 5.3 below.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1. Confidential Information. Except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or otherwise agreed to orally by the parties, each party agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for 20 years thereafter, it shall keep confidential and shall not publish or otherwise disclose and shall not use for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement any Confidential Information furnished to it by the other party pursuant to this Agreement, except that the foregoing shall not apply to any information for which the receiving party can demonstrate that such information: (i) was already known to the receiving party, other than under an obligation of confidentiality, at the time of disclosure by the other party; (ii) was generally available to the public or otherwise part of the public domain at the time of its disclosure to the receiving party; (iii) later became part of the public domain through no act or omission of the receiving party; (iv) was disclosed to the receiving party by a Third Party who had no obligation to the disclosing party not to disclose such information to others; (iv) was independently developed by a person having no knowledge of or access to the disclosing party's Confidential Information; or (v) is an Authorized Disclosure under Section 5.3 below.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that any authorization or agreement related to confidential information must be in writing. Modifying it to allow for oral agreements contradicts the need for written consent and generates uncertainty about enforcement, as it would be difficult to verify the agreement was made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.7. Modification. No change, modification, addition or amendment to this Agreement is valid or enforceable unless in writing and signed and dated by the authorized officers of the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that any modifications to the confidentiality agreement must be in writing. The changed text allows for oral agreements regarding confidential information. This directly contradicts section 12.7, which states, 'No change, modification, addition or amendment to this Agreement is valid or enforceable unless in writing and signed and dated by the authorized officers of the parties to this Agreement.' The changed text introduces a clear contradiction regarding the validity of oral modifications, making enforcement uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: 8.5. Enforcement against Third Parties. (a) Notice. If either party learns of the actual, suspected, threatened or likely infringement or misappropriation of any of the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology, or any of the Integra Technology, then that party shall give written notice thereof to the other party and shall provide the other party with any evidence of such infringement or misappropriation in its possession.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.5. Enforcement against Third Parties. (a) Notice. If either party learns of the actual, suspected, threatened or likely infringement or misappropriation of any of the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology, or any of the Integra Technology, then that party shall give verbal notice thereof to the other party and shall provide the other party with any evidence of such infringement or misappropriation in its possession.
perturbation.explanation: The original text dictates that notice of infringement or misappropriation should be given in writing. The altered text requires verbal notification instead. This contradicts section 12.1 that states all official notices be in writing.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
12.1. Notices. Any notice, request, instruction or other document required or permitted to be given under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been given (i) if mailed with the United States Postal Service by prepaid, first class, certified mail, return receipt requested, at the time of receipt by the intended recipient, (ii) if sent by Federal Express, Airborne, or other overnight carrier, signature of delivery required, at the time of receipt by the intended recipient, (iii) if sent by facsimile transmission, when so sent and when receipt has been acknowledged by appropriate telephone or facsimile receipt, or (iv) if hand-delivered, at the time of receipt by the intended recipient, addressed as follows:
(a) For Integra:
Brian Larkin, President, Global Spine and Orthobiologics

perturbation.justification: The original text states that notice of infringement should be 'written'. The changed text requires 'verbal' notification. Section 12.1 states 'Any notice... shall be in writing'. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts section 12.1, because it specifies a method of notification (verbal) that is explicitly disallowed by section 12.1.
